title: Firmware woes for RAK14000 E-Paper screen (Adafruit SSD1680)

messages.author: Dakota Sea Witch
messages.text: Hey yall, has anyone by chance gotten the RAK E-Paper screen to work with any firmware or builds? I'm running an nRF52 board (RAK4631, aka nrf52840_dk I believe), and have tried so many different build tests to see if my screen will even display a simple test, and nothing... In theory, this sample code provided by Adafruit should work... But nope. https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_EPD/blob/master/src/drivers/Adafruit_SSD1680.cpp I'm pulling my hair out at this point. Any and all advice/support/questions would be welcome. Build Info: IDE = VS Code (with PlatformIO, project configs have the correct port, COM7, defined to communicate with my build) Platform = nordicnrf52 Board = nrf52840_dk (aka RAK4631) Framework = Arduino Libraries = Adafruit EPD, Adafruit GFX, GxEPD2.
messages.content_html:
Hey yall, has anyone by chance gotten the RAK E-Paper screen to work with any firmware or builds? I'm running an nRF52 board (RAK4631, aka nrf52840_dk I believe), and have tried so many different build tests to see if my screen will even display a simple test, and nothing... In theory, this sample code provided by Adafruit should work... But nope. https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_EPD/blob/master/src/drivers/Adafruit_SSD1680.cpp I'm pulling my hair out at this point. Any and all advice/support/questions would be welcome. Build Info:
  • IDE = VS Code (with PlatformIO, project configs have the correct port, COM7, defined to communicate with my build)
  • Platform = nordicnrf52
  • Board = nrf52840_dk (aka RAK4631)
  • Framework = Arduino
  • Libraries = Adafruit EPD, Adafruit GFX, GxEPD2.
